description: SUMO (yeast Smt3, S. cerevisiae) — 98-aa ubiquitin-like beta-grasp protein ending in the conserved C-terminal diglycine; canonical N-terminal SUMO-fusion solubility/expression tag cleaved at the Gly-Gly by the SUMO protease Ulp1 to release a native N-terminus.

label: SUMO
note: Small ubiquitin-related modifier (Smt3 / SUMO), Saccharomyces cerevisiae SMT3; UniProt Q12306, residues 1–98. Ubiquitin-like (UBL) protein with a beta-grasp fold. As stored this is the mature/processed form retaining the initiator Met (M1) for use as a recombinant N-terminal fusion tag and ending at the conserved C-terminal Gly-Gly (G97-G98), the Ulp1 cleavage site. The full precursor is 101 aa; the C-terminal propeptide (residues 99–101, ATY) is removed by the SUMO protease Ulp1 to expose the Gly-Gly. Widely deployed as a SUMO-fusion partner that enhances soluble expression of difficult passengers and, upon Ulp1 cleavage at the Gly-Gly, releases the passenger with a native N-terminus.

provenance.sequence_source: UniProt:Q12306 (SUMO_YEAST / SMT3, S. cerevisiae), residues 1–98 of 101; the stored 98-aa sequence is 100% identical to Q12306[1:98] — independently fetched via https://rest.uniprot.org/uniprotkb/Q12306.fasta and compared byte-for-byte (0 mismatches). Residues 99–101 (ATY) are the annotated C-terminal propeptide (FT PROPEP 99..101) cleaved by Ulp1; the part correctly ends at G98, the Ulp1 cleavage site.

functional_claims.label: SUMO is an N-terminal fusion partner that enhances soluble expression and overall expression yield of fused (including difficult-to-express) proteins in E. coli

source.quote: SUMO fusions and SUMO-specific protease for efficient expression and purification of proteins

functional_claims.label: The SUMO tag is removed by the SUMO-specific protease Ulp1, which cleaves at the C-terminal Gly-Gly to release the passenger with a native N-terminus

value.recognition: Ulp1 recognizes the folded 3D structure of SUMO, not a linear sequence motif
value.product: passenger protein with a native (scarless) N-terminus
value.p1prime_constraint: any residue tolerated at the +1 position except proline

source.quote: SUMO fusion technology for difficult-to-express proteins

functional_claims.label: Ulp1 cleavage requires the conserved C-terminal Gly-Gly; single substitutions are tolerated but dual mutation of the diglycine abolishes cleavage

value.structural_basis: tapered active-site pocket of Ulp1 accommodates the diglycine

source.quote: Profiling Substrate Specificity of the SUMO Protease Ulp1 by the YESS-PSSC System to Advance the Conserved Mechanism for Substrate Cleavage

functional_claims.label: In a head-to-head comparison of fusion partners, SUMO ranked among the highest for enhancing expression and solubility, comparable to NusA and ahead of MBP, GST, TRX and ubiquitin

value.ranking: highest tier (with NusA) for expression/solubility among MBP, GST, TRX, Ub, NusA, SUMO
value.protease_kinetics: Ulp1 kcat ~25-fold higher than AcTEV protease
value.cleavage_product: native N-terminus on the released passenger

source.quote: Comparison of SUMO fusion technology with traditional gene fusion systems: enhanced expression and solubility with SUMO

functional_claims.label: Native to Saccharomyces cerevisiae (SMT3 gene product); deployed as a fusion tag primarily in E. coli expression systems

source.quote: SUMO fusions and SUMO-specific protease for efficient expression and purification of proteins
